content: The intrinsic reward prediction errors that power curiosity-driven methods like ICM and RND can be repurposed as a dynamic memory pruning signal for state-of-the-art active learning agents, where only surprising, prediction-violating experiences are retained in long-term episodic memory. This creates a self-sustaining cycle where the agent’s curiosity directly shapes the size and relevance of its memory buffer, eliminating redundant low-information traces without manual tuning.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
